title: Patreon moves from requesting to enforcing limits on AI training crawlers
friendlyTitle: Patreon starts actively blocking AI training bots
summary: Patreon is expanding its use of Cloudflare AI Crawl Control to block known AI training crawlers at the network level while continuing to allow search crawlers that send visitors back to creators. Patreon says early tests reduced weekly access attempts by individual training crawlers from thousands to zero.
friendlySummary: Patreon is using Cloudflare tools to block bots that collect creators’ work for AI training. Search bots that help people find creators can still be allowed.
whyItMatters: For creators, the change turns a voluntary robots.txt request into a technical control while preserving ordinary discovery. It also illustrates a broader shift toward separating search indexing from model training rather than treating every crawler the same.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Creators get more control without disappearing from search. Other websites may use a similar approach instead of only asking bots to behave.
